title: Reload provider config every time Provider Configuration tab is accessed
description:
**Problem:**
Provider configuration is loaded only once. After saving, the backend deletes the config CR, so saving multiple attributes without reloading causes exceptions.

**Requirements:**
EVERY time the user clicks on the "Provider Configuration" tab (eventKey 2), re-execute ALL API calls:
1. Reset provider config state to idle
2. Create new provider config request
3. Trigger schema polling

**Current Code (Settings.tsx lines 66-93):**
```tsx
if (
  activeTabKey === 2 &&
  state.providers && 
  state.providers.length > 0 &&
  state.providerConfigStatus === 'idle'  // ← Prevents reload!
)
```

**Solution:**
1. Track previous activeTabKey with useRef
2. When activeTabKey changes TO 2:
   - Dispatch PROVIDER_CONFIG_RESET action
   - Create new config request (even if status not idle)

**Files to Modify:**
- src/components/Settings.tsx
- src/context/AppContext.tsx (add PROVIDER_CONFIG_RESET if needed)

**Testing:**
1. Open Provider Configuration tab → loads
2. Save a config
3. Switch to another tab
4. Return to Provider Configuration → fresh reload

title: Add tests for active runs dashboard components
description: Write unit tests for: useActiveRunsPoller hook (polling behavior, cleanup), ActiveRunsSummary component (rendering, icon click), ActiveRunsModal component (tree view rendering, close). Use React Testing Library and vitest.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive tests for active runs dashboard components: useActiveRunsPoller hook (3 tests), ActiveRunsSummary (8 tests), and ActiveRunsModal (6 tests). All 17 tests passing.

- Route guards:

- Navigation:
  - Add "Users" link to main navigation menu (admin only visible)
  - Add "My Profile" link to user dropdown menu (all authenticated users)
  - Breadcrumb navigation on user pages

- 404 handling:
  - User not found -> show 404 page
  - Permission denied -> show 403 page

Technical:
- Use React Router v6
- Protected route components
- useAuth hook for permission checks
- Redirect to login if not authenticated
- Redirect to forbidden if no permission

Files to modify:

- src/components/Navigation/MainNav.tsx

Files to create:

```

title: Frontend: User CRUD API - Architecture Overview
description:
# User Management API Overview

The backend has implemented a complete REST API for user management. This guide documents API behavior for frontend implementation.

## Endpoint Structure

**Base path**: `/api/v1/users` (NOT /api/v1/auth/users)

**Authentication**: All endpoints require JWT token in header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

## Available Endpoints

1. **GET /api/v1/users** - List users (admin only)
2. **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Get user details (admin or self)
3. **POST /api/v1/users** - Create user (admin only)
4. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId** - Update profile (admin or self)
5. **DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId** - Delete user (admin only)
6. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Change password (admin or self)

## ⚠️ CRITICAL: Two Different PATCH Endpoints

There are TWO different PATCH endpoints for the same user:

1. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId` - Updates PROFILE (name, surname, org, role, active)
2. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password` - Changes PASSWORD

**Do NOT confuse them!** Password is NOT updated via profile PATCH.

## Permission Matrix

| Action | Admin | User (self) | User (others) |
|--------|-------|-------------|---------------|
| List users | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View user | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |
| Create user | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update name/surname/org | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |
| Update role/active | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete user | ✅ | ❌ (not even self) | ❌ |
| Change password | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |

## Backend Security Rules

1. **Cannot delete self**: Even admin cannot delete their own account
2. **Cannot delete last admin**: Backend prevents deleting the last active admin
3. **Current password required**: Users must provide current password to change it
4. **Admin bypass**: Admin does NOT need current password to change other users' passwords

## Frontend Implementation Required

Create:
- UserService for API calls
- Permission guards/checks based on role
- Separate forms for update profile vs change password
- Client-side validation matching backend rules
- Error handling for all cases (403, 404, 409, etc.)

## Related Tasks

See individual implementation tasks for each endpoint.

title: Fix ARIA relationships broken by conditional rendering
description: Using conditional rendering for DataListContent removes the element from DOM when closed, breaking aria-controls relationship. Need hybrid approach: always render DataListContent (for ARIA id) but conditionally render internal content (to prevent LogViewer mounting).
close_reason: Fixed with hybrid approach: DataListContent always rendered (preserves ARIA id) but internal content conditionally rendered (prevents LogViewer mounting). Applied to both accordion levels.

title: Create CreateGroupModal component
description:
Modal for creating new groups with cluster permissions.

**Fields:**
- Group Name (required, text input)
- Description (optional, textarea)

**Cluster Selection:**
1. On modal open: fetch GET /api/v1/operator/targets
2. Parse response to flatten cluster list:
   - Iterate targets[].providers[].clusters[]
   - Extract: clusterName, clusterAPIURL
3. Display table/list with:
   - Column 1: Cluster Name (e.g., 'prod-cluster-1')
   - Column 2: API Endpoint (e.g., 'https://api.cluster1.example.com')
   - Column 3-5: Checkboxes for actions (View, Run, Cancel)
4. User selects which actions for each cluster

**Submission:**
- Build clusterPermissions object:
  {
    'https://api.cluster1.example.com': { actions: ['view', 'run'] },
    'https://api.cluster2.example.com': { actions: ['view', 'run', 'cancel'] }
  }
- Call createGroup({ name, description, clusterPermissions })
- On success: close modal, refresh groups list, show success toast
- On error: show validation errors inline

**Validation:**
- Name required, no spaces/special chars (sanitized on backend)
- At least one cluster must have at least one action selected

**UI/UX:**
- Use Patternfly Modal component
- Clear cluster table with sortable columns
- Checkbox tri-state if needed (all/some/none per cluster)

title: Fix scrollbars appearing when user menu opens
description: When the user menu dropdown is opened, horizontal and vertical scrollbars appear on the page, causing an unwanted layout shift. The dropdown should not affect the page layout or cause overflow.

title: Implement User Details page
description:
Create a detailed view page for individual user information.

Requirements:
- Display all user information:
  - Email (primary identifier)
  - Full name (Name + Surname)
  - Organization
  - Role with badge (admin/user)
  - Active status with indicator
  - Created date (formatted)
  - Last login date (formatted, or 'Never' if null)
- Action buttons (permission-based):
  - Edit button (admin or own profile)
  - Delete button (admin only, not yourself)
  - Activate/Deactivate toggle (admin only)
- Activity section:
  - Recent login history (if available from backend)
  - Created chaos scenarios count (if available)
- Breadcrumb navigation (Users > user@email.com)
- Back to users list button
- 404 page if user not found

API endpoint: GET /api/v1/users/:userId (needs to be implemented in backend)

Technical:
- Use React Router params to get userId
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Error state (not found, permission denied)
- Responsive layout
- Auto-refresh on interval (optional, for last login)

Files to create:
- src/pages/Users/UserDetails.tsx
- src/components/Users/UserInfo.tsx
- src/components/Users/UserActivitySection.tsx

title: Add cluster conflict check before scenario launch
description: When user initiates scenario run, call /api/v1/dashboard/active-runs to check if target cluster has active runs. If cluster exists in clusterRuns map, show ClusterConflictWarning modal instead of proceeding directly. Pass cluster name and existing run names to modal.

title: Frontend: User CRUD API - Overview e Architettura
description:
# Overview API User Management

Il backend ha implementato un'API REST completa per la gestione utenti. Questa guida documenta il comportamento dell'API per l'implementazione frontend.

## Struttura Endpoints

**Base path**: `/api/v1/users` (NON /api/v1/auth/users)

**Authentication**: Tutti gli endpoint richiedono JWT token nell'header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

## Endpoints Disponibili

1. **GET /api/v1/users** - Lista utenti (admin only)
2. **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Dettagli utente (admin or self)
3. **POST /api/v1/users** - Crea utente (admin only)
4. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId** - Aggiorna profilo (admin or self)
5. **DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId** - Elimina utente (admin only)
6. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Cambia password (admin or self)

## ⚠️ IMPORTANTE: Due Endpoint PATCH Diversi

Ci sono DUE endpoint PATCH per lo stesso user:

1. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId` - Aggiorna PROFILO (nome, cognome, org, role, active)
2. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password` - Cambia PASSWORD

**NON confonderli!** La password NON si aggiorna con il PATCH del profilo.

## Permission Matrix

| Azione | Admin | User (self) | User (others) |
|--------|-------|-------------|---------------|
| List users | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View user | ✅ | ✅ (solo proprio) | ❌ |
| Create user | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update name/surname/org | ✅ | ✅ (solo proprio) | ❌ |
| Update role/active | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete user | ✅ | ❌ (neanche se stesso) | ❌ |
| Change password | ✅ | ✅ (solo propria) | ❌ |

## Regole di Sicurezza Backend

1. **Non può cancellare se stesso**: Anche admin non può cancellare il proprio account
2. **Non può cancellare ultimo admin**: Backend impedisce di eliminare l'ultimo admin attivo
3. **Password corrente richiesta**: User deve fornire password corrente per cambiarla
4. **Admin può cambiare password altrui**: Admin NON serve password corrente per altri utenti

## Implementazione Frontend

Creare:
- UserService per chiamate API
- Permission guards/checks basati su role
- Form separati per update profilo vs change password
- Validazione client-side che rispetti le regole backend
- Error handling per tutti i casi (403, 404, 409, etc.)

## Task Correlati

- Frontend: Implementare lista utenti (GET /users)
- Frontend: Implementare form crea utente (POST /users)
- Frontend: Implementare form modifica profilo (PATCH /users/:id)
- Frontend: Implementare form cambia password (PATCH /users/:id/password)
- Frontend: Implementare elimina utente (DELETE /users/:id)

title: Improve provider config loading spinner to block interactions
description:
**Problem:** Current loading spinner for provider config is: - Not visually prominent enough - Doesn't prevent user interaction with table rows - Looks "brutto" (ugly) **Requirements:** Create a full-page/overlay spinner that: 1. **Covers the entire provider config area** (not just a small spinner) 2. **Blocks all interactions** with accordion/table during loading 3. **More visually appealing** and obvious 4. Shows during: - `providerConfigStatus === 'creating'` - `providerConfigStatus === 'polling'` **Design Options:** **Option 1: Backdrop Spinner (Recommended)** ```tsx {(state.providerConfigStatus === 'creating' || state.providerConfigStatus === 'polling') && (
{state.providerConfigStatus === 'creating' ? 'Initializing configuration...' : 'Loading provider schemas...'}
)} ``` **Option 2: PatternFly Bullseye + Overlay** ```tsx
{loading && (
)} ...
``` **Files to Modify:** - src/components/Settings.tsx (Provider Configuration tab section, lines 192-209) **Current Code to Replace (lines 192-203):** ```tsx {state.providerConfigStatus === 'creating' && ( Creating configuration request... )} {state.providerConfigStatus === 'polling' && ( Collecting configuration schemas from active providers... )} ``` **Testing:** 1. Navigate to Provider Configuration tab 2. Verify full-page spinner appears 3. Try to click on accordion items → should be blocked 4. Wait for schemas to load → spinner disappears 5. Accordion is now interactive

title: Prevent duplicate WebSocket connections in React StrictMode
description: React StrictMode causes LogViewer useEffect to run twice in dev mode, creating duplicate WebSocket connections. Need to implement connection guard pattern to prevent double connection while keeping StrictMode benefits.
close_reason: Fixed with WebSocket readyState guard. Checks if connection already exists before creating new one, preventing StrictMode double-mount from creating duplicate connections.

title: Add logs view for Succeeded/Failed pods in job accordion
Fix login redirect loop causing infinite spinner
description:
After successful login, user was stuck in infinite redirect loop between / and /login causing continuous spinner and repeated isRegistered API calls.

Root cause: Login redirected to / which triggered RegistrationCheck, which then redirected back to /login, creating infinite loop.

Fix: 
- RegistrationCheck now checks if user is authenticated first and redirects to /app
- Login page default returnUrl changed from / to /app
- Prevents redirect loop for authenticated users

Commit: 2b1d322

title: Parallelize multi-arch builds in release workflow (Console)
description:
Apply parallelization strategy to krkn-operator-console release workflow if applicable.

## Reference Implementation
krkn-operator (tsebastiani-16i) - Parallelized 4 builds:
- Result: 30min → 15min (50% faster)

## Current State (Console)
Frontend React/Next.js project - investigate:
- Does console have Docker image builds?
- Does it build multi-arch (amd64, arm64)?
- Is there a release workflow?

## Possible Scenarios

### Scenario A: Multi-Arch Container Builds
If console builds Docker images for multiple platforms:
- Apply same parallelization pattern as krkn-operator
- Build amd64 and arm64 in parallel
- Create multi-arch manifest

### Scenario B: Frontend-Only Deployment
If console is deployed as static files:
- No Docker build needed
- May need build optimization for assets
- Parallel build of different deployment targets?

### Scenario C: No Release Workflow
If no automated release:
- Document manual release process
- Consider adding CI/CD pipeline
- Close task as not applicable

## Implementation Steps
1. [ ] Check if `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists
2. [ ] Identify what gets built/released
3. [ ] Determine if parallelization applies
4. [ ] If yes: apply same pattern as krkn-operator
5. [ ] If no: document and close task

## Notes
- Frontend projects may have different build patterns
- Static assets vs containerized deployment
- May need different optimization strategy than backend
- Check deployment target (Netlify, Vercel, k8s, etc.)

title: Frontend: Implement Update User Profile (PATCH /users/:id)
description:
# Update User Profile Endpoint

**Endpoint**: `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId`

**⚠️ IMPORTANT**: This endpoint updates profile fields ONLY. For password, use separate endpoint `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password`

## Authorization

- **Admin**: Can update any user (all fields)
- **User**: Can update own profile only (name, surname, organization)
- **User cannot update**: role, active (admin only fields)

## Request

**Method**: `PATCH` (partial update - send only fields you want to change)

**Headers**:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
Content-Type: application/json
```

**Body** (all fields optional):
```json
{
  "name": "Updated Name",
  "surname": "Updated Surname",
  "organization": "New Organization",
  "role": "admin",      // Admin only
  "active": true          // Admin only
}
```

## Response

**Success (200)**:
```json
{
  "message": "User updated successfully",
  "user": {
    "userId": "user@example.com",
    "name": "Updated Name",
    "surname": "Updated Surname",
    "organization": "New Organization",
    "role": "user",
    "active": true,
    "created": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "lastLogin": "2025-01-20T15:30:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Error Responses

**400 - Validation Error**:
```json
{
  "error": "validation_error",
  "message": "At least one field must be provided"
}
```

```json
{
  "error": "validation_error",
  "message": "Role must be either 'user' or 'admin'"
}
```

**403 - Forbidden**:
```json
{
  "error": "forbidden",
  "message": "You can only update your own profile"
}
```

```json
{
  "error": "forbidden",
  "message": "Only admins can change role or active status"
}
```

**404 - Not Found**:
```json
{
  "error": "not_found",
  "message": "user with email 'user@example.com' not found"
}
```

## Frontend Implementation

### User Profile Edit Form

**For regular users**:
- Show fields: name, surname, organization
- Hide/disable: role, active (read-only display)
- Can only edit own profile

**For admins**:
- Show all fields: name, surname, organization, role, active
- Can edit any user

### Validation

Client-side validation:
- At least one field must be changed
- Role must be "user" or "admin" if provided
- Prevent users from changing role/active (hide/disable inputs)

### Example Implementation

```typescript
async function updateUserProfile(userId: string, updates: Partial<UserProfile>) {
  const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/users/${userId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(updates)
  });
  
  if (!response.ok) {
    const error = await response.json();
    throw new Error(error.message);
  }
  
  return await response.json();
}

// Usage - Update own profile
await updateUserProfile('me@example.com', {
  name: 'New Name',
  surname: 'New Surname'
});

// Usage - Admin updates user role
await updateUserProfile('user@example.com', {
  role: 'admin',
  active: true
});
```

## Testing Scenarios

1. User updates own name/surname - should succeed
2. User tries to update own role - should fail 403
3. User tries to update other user - should fail 403
4. Admin updates any user's role - should succeed
5. Admin updates user's active status - should succeed
6. Update with empty body - should fail 400
7. Update non-existent user - should fail 404

## UI/UX Considerations

- Show different form fields based on user role
- Disable role/active fields for non-admins
- Show success message after update
- Refresh user data after successful update
- Handle errors gracefully with user-friendly messages
- Consider "Save" button state (disabled until changes made)

## Related Endpoints

- **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Get user details before editing
- **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Separate endpoint for password (DO NOT CONFUSE)

title: Create ClusterConflictWarning modal component

title: Add confirmation popup after provider config save with home/reload options
description:
**Problem:**
After saving a provider configuration, the backend deletes the config CR. Users need to reload before making another change.

**Requirements:**
When a provider configuration is successfully saved, show a Modal popup:
- **Title:** "Configuration Applied"
- **Message:** "Changes have been applied successfully. Do you want to return to the home page?"
- **Buttons:**
  - **Yes** → Navigate to home (/)
  - **No** → Stay on page AND reload provider config (same as tab re-entry)

**Implementation Details:**

**Modal Popup:**
```tsx
<Modal
  variant={ModalVariant.small}
  title="Configuration Applied"
  isOpen={showSaveSuccessModal}
  onClose={() => setShowSaveSuccessModal(false)}
  actions={[
    <Button key="home" variant="primary" onClick={handleGoHome}>
      Yes, go to Home
    </Button>,
    <Button key="reload" variant="secondary" onClick={handleReload}>
      No, reload configuration
    </Button>
  ]}
>
  Changes have been applied successfully. Do you want to return to the home page?
</Modal>
```

**Handlers:**
- `handleGoHome`: `navigate('/')`
- `handleReload`: Reset provider config state + create new request (same as tab re-entry)

**Files to Modify:**
- src/components/ProviderConfigTab.tsx (show modal after successful save)
- OR Settings.tsx (if handling at parent level)

**User Flow:**
1. User edits provider config
2. Clicks "Save Configuration"
3. Success → Modal appears
4. User chooses:
   - "Yes" → Redirected to home
   - "No" → Modal closes, config reloads, ready for next edit

title: Implement Users API client
description:
Create API client functions for user CRUD operations.

Requirements:
- Implement API functions:
  - listUsers(filters?: {role?: string, active?: boolean, search?: string}) - GET /api/v1/users
  - getUserById(userId: string) - GET /api/v1/users/:userId
  - createUser(userData: CreateUserRequest) - POST /api/v1/users
  - updateUser(userId: string, updates: UpdateUserRequest) - PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId
  - deleteUser(userId: string) - DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId
  - activateUser(userId: string) - PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/activate
  - deactivateUser(userId: string) - PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/deactivate

- TypeScript interfaces:
  - User (response model)
  - CreateUserRequest
  - UpdateUserRequest
  - UsersListResponse (with pagination)

- Authentication:
  - Include JWT token in Authorization header
  - Handle 401 (redirect to login)
  - Handle 403 (permission denied)

- Error handling:
  - Wrap API errors with custom error class
  - Map HTTP status codes to user-friendly messages
  - Network error handling

- Request/Response interceptors:
  - Add auth token automatically
  - Parse error responses
  - Log API calls (dev mode)

Technical:
- Use axios or fetch
- TypeScript for type safety
- Retry logic for failed requests (optional)
- Request cancellation support
- Mock API responses for development (optional)

Files to create:
- src/api/users.ts
- src/types/user.ts
- src/api/errors.ts (if not exists)

title: Group cluster selection: add operator grouping and duplicate detection
description: 1. Group clusters by operator source (krkn-operator, krkn-operator-acm) in ClusterPermissionsTable. 2. Add validation to detect and warn when same cluster (same API URL) is selected from multiple sources.
close_reason: Implemented operator grouping in ClusterPermissionsTable and duplicate cluster detection in group modals. Clusters now grouped visually by operator source. Duplicate validation warns when same API URL selected from multiple operators. All tests passing.

title: Implement Quake-style dropdown terminal - Step 1: Basic structure
description: Create dropdown terminal with trigger strip below topbar. Configurable height (default 1/4 screen), opaque backdrop, slide-down animation. This is step 1: just the UI shell with open/close functionality.

title: Fix websocket connections created for closed job accordions
description: WebSocket connections are being created for ALL jobs even when their accordions are closed. This happens because DataListContent uses isHidden prop which only hides with CSS but still mounts the LogViewer component. Need to replace with conditional rendering to only mount LogViewer when accordion is open.
close_reason: Fixed by replacing DataListContent isHidden prop with conditional rendering. LogViewer now only mounts when accordion is expanded, preventing unnecessary WebSocket connections.

title: Implement API calls for Groups (list, create, delete)
description:
Implement all API client functions for Groups management:

**Groups CRUD:**
- listGroups() → GET /api/v1/groups
- getGroup(name) → GET /api/v1/groups/:groupName
- createGroup(data) → POST /api/v1/groups
  - Body: { name, description, clusterPermissions: { [apiUrl]: { actions: ['view','run','cancel'] } } }
- updateGroup(name, data) → PATCH /api/v1/groups/:groupName
  - Body: { description?, clusterPermissions? }
- deleteGroup(name) → DELETE /api/v1/groups/:groupName

**Group Members:**
- listGroupMembers(groupName) → GET /api/v1/groups/:groupName/members
- addGroupMember(groupName, userId) → POST /api/v1/groups/:groupName/members
  - Body: { userId: 'user@example.com' }
- removeGroupMember(groupName, userId) → DELETE /api/v1/groups/:groupName/members/:userId

**Targets (for cluster selection):**
- listTargets() → GET /api/v1/operator/targets
  - Returns: [{ uuid, name, providers: { name: 'provider', clusters: [{ clusterName, clusterAPIURL }] } }]

**Integration:**
- Use existing API client pattern (check src/api/ or src/services/)
- Add React Query hooks if used in project
- Handle authentication (JWT token)
- Error handling with toast notifications

title: Frontend: User cannot disable own account
Frontend: Implement List Users (GET /users)
Debug QuakeTerminal trigger strip not visible
description: Trigger strip below masthead not showing. Need to check: 1) PatternFly Masthead actual height, 2) z-index conflicts, 3) Page component layout covering the strip

title: Add multi-architecture Docker builds with automatic platform selection
description:
Update release workflow to build and push multi-arch images:

- Architectures: linux/amd64, linux/arm64
- Use docker buildx with manifest lists (NO separate tags per architecture)
- Registry automatically serves correct image based on platform
- Image: krkn-operator-console

Implementation:
- Set up QEMU and docker buildx
- Use docker/build-push-action with platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
- Single tag push creates multi-arch manifest
- Registry (Quay.io) automatically selects correct architecture on pull

Example: `docker pull quay.io/krkn-chaos/krkn-operator-console:v1.0.0`
→ ARM system gets ARM image, x86 system gets x86 image automatically

Align with krkn-operator implementation.

Reference: https://docs.docker.com/build/building/multi-platform/
close_reason: Already implemented - console workflow already has multi-arch builds with QEMU, buildx, and linux/amd64,linux/arm64 platforms

title: Add group selection to CreateUserModal
description:
Add group membership selection to user creation flow.

**CreateUserModal Updates:**
1. Add new field: 'Groups' (multi-select)
2. On modal open: receive groups array as prop from parent
3. Display groups as multi-select dropdown/checkboxes:
   - Show group name and member count
   - e.g., 'dev-team (5 members)', 'ops-team (3 members)'
4. User selects one or more groups (required, at least one)

**Form Validation:**
- Groups field is required
- Show error if submitted without selecting groups: 'Select at least one group'

**Submission Flow:**
Two approaches:

**Option A (Recommended): Sequential API calls**
1. POST /api/v1/users (create user) → get created user
2. For each selected group:
   - POST /api/v1/groups/{groupName}/members with userId
3. Show combined success/error feedback

**Option B: Backend handles it**
- Extend POST /api/v1/users to accept groups array
- Backend creates user + adds to groups in transaction
- Requires backend API changes

**Recommendation: Use Option A**
- No backend changes needed
- Leverage existing group membership API
- Handle partial failures gracefully (user created but some group adds failed)

**Error Handling:**
- If user creation fails: abort, show error
- If user created but group add fails:
  - User exists but not in all groups
  - Show warning: 'User created but failed to add to 2 groups. Add manually.'
  - Or: rollback user creation (DELETE user) and retry
- Best UX: show which groups succeeded/failed

**UI/UX:**
- Use Patternfly Select (multi-select mode)
- Show selected count: '3 groups selected'
- Visual feedback during async operations
- Success: 'User created and added to 3 groups'
- Partial failure: 'User created but only added to 1 of 3 groups'

title: Create useActiveRunsPoller hook with 2s polling
description: Custom React hook that polls /api/v1/dashboard/active-runs every 2 seconds. Should handle loading, error states, and cleanup on unmount. Returns activeRuns state.

title: Fix: User displays as 'Unknown' when job transitions to Running state
description: Bug: When a scenario run transitions from Pending to Running, the ownerUserId field is lost during polling updates, causing the user to display as 'Unknown' in the UI. Fixed by preserving ownerUserId in both useScenarioRunsPoller and useTargetPoller hooks.
close_reason: Fixed by preserving ownerUserId in polling hooks (useScenarioRunsPoller and useTargetPoller)

title: Implement Edit User page
description:
Create a page to edit existing user information.

Requirements:
- Pre-populate form with current user data
- Editable fields:
  - Name
  - Surname
  - Organization
  - Role (admin only can change roles)
  - Active status (admin only can activate/deactivate users)
- Password change section (optional):
  - Current password (if editing own account)
  - New password
  - Confirm new password
- Cannot edit email (read-only)
- Regular users can only edit their own profile
- Admin users can edit any user
- Show last login timestamp (read-only)
- Show created date (read-only)
- Save/Cancel buttons
- Confirmation dialog before deactivating user
- Success message on save
- Error handling

API endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId (needs to be implemented in backend)

Technical:
- Reuse UserForm component from Create User
- Load user data on mount
- Handle permission checks (can edit own profile, admin can edit anyone)
- Optimistic updates with rollback on error
- Dirty form detection (warn on navigate away with unsaved changes)

Files to create:
- src/pages/Users/EditUser.tsx
- src/components/Users/PasswordChangeSection.tsx
- src/hooks/useUserPermissions.ts

title: Standardize Makefile for krkn-operator-console with Docker/Podman support
description:
Bonifica Makefile per rilascio beta v1:

- Add CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker variable
- Add dynamic registry variables with COMPONENT=krkn-operator-console
- Add REGISTRY ?= quay.io/krkn-chaos
- Add IMG_NAME ?= $(COMPONENT)
- Add git tag detection: GIT_TAG ?= $(shell git describe --tags --exact-match 2>/dev/null)
- Add IMG_TAG ?= latest
- Update IMG to use: IMG ?= $(REGISTRY)/$(IMG_NAME):$(IMG_TAG)
- Update docker-build to tag both latest and git tag (use CONTAINER_TOOL)
- Update docker-push to push both latest and git tag (use CONTAINER_TOOL)
- Add podman-build wrapper: make docker-build CONTAINER_TOOL=podman
- Add podman-push wrapper: make docker-push CONTAINER_TOOL=podman
- Add NAMESPACE ?= krkn-operator-system variable
- Update deploy to create namespace if missing
- Verify IMG points to quay.io/krkn-chaos/krkn-operator-console:latest

Test checklist:
- make docker-build && make docker-push
- make podman-build && make podman-push
- make deploy NAMESPACE=test-console
- Verify image tags correctly

title: User Management

close_reason: User Management completamente implementato: lista/create/edit/delete/view users, change password, safety checks, RBAC, integrato in Settings → User Settings tab. Test coverage 100% (47 test passing). API client completo per tutti gli endpoint /users.

title: Add tests for cluster conflict warning feature
description: Write unit tests for: ClusterConflictWarning modal (rendering, cancel/continue buttons), conflict check logic in scenario launch workflow. Mock API calls. Verify modal shows/hides correctly.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive tests for ClusterConflictWarning component (9 tests covering modal states, singular/plural messages, button callbacks, and display elements). All tests passing.

title: Fix scenario run accordion also uses isHidden (nested websocket leak)
description: Parent scenario run accordion still uses isHidden prop. When closed, it hides but doesn't unmount child job accordions. If a job accordion was expanded, its LogViewer remains mounted with active WebSocket. Need to apply same conditional rendering fix to scenario run level.
close_reason: Fixed by applying conditional rendering to scenario run accordion. Now both parent and child accordions properly unmount LogViewer components, ensuring WebSocket cleanup at all nesting levels.

title: Frontend: Password change uses separate endpoint
description:
# Password Change Behavior

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: Password changes use a SEPARATE endpoint from profile updates.

## Two Different PATCH Endpoints

When updating a user, there are TWO different PATCH endpoints:

### 1. Update Profile (PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId)
- Updates: name, surname, organization, role*, active*
- Does NOT update password
- Use this for profile edits

### 2. Change Password (PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password)
- Updates: password only
- Does NOT update profile fields
- Use this for password changes

## Password Change Request Body

**User changing own password** (requires current password):
```json
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass123",
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

**Admin changing other user's password** (no current password needed):
```json
{
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

## Key Differences from Profile Update

1. **Different URL**: Append `/password` to the user URL
2. **Different body**: Uses currentPassword/newPassword fields, not profile fields
3. **Different validation**: Requires current password for self (not for admin)
4. **Cannot mix**: Cannot send password field to profile endpoint or vice versa

## Implementation Notes

- Create separate form/modal for password change
- Do NOT add password field to profile edit form
- Check if user is admin to show/hide currentPassword field
- Validate password strength client-side (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number)
- Show success message after password change
- Consider forcing re-login after password change

## Example API Calls

```typescript
// WRONG - This will NOT change password
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com
{
  "name": "New Name",
  "password": "NewPass123"  // ❌ Ignored!
}

// CORRECT - Profile update
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com
{
  "name": "New Name",
  "surname": "New Surname"
}

// CORRECT - Password change
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com/password
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass123",
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

title: Create EditGroupModal with cluster permissions editing
description:
Modal for editing existing group permissions.

**Pre-population:**
1. Receive groupName as prop
2. On open: fetch GET /api/v1/groups/:groupName
3. Pre-fill form with existing data:
   - Name: display read-only (cannot be changed)
   - Description: editable textarea
   - Cluster permissions: restore checkbox state from clusterPermissions object

**Cluster Selection (same as Create):**
1. Fetch GET /api/v1/operator/targets
2. Parse and flatten cluster list
3. Display table with: Cluster Name, API Endpoint, View/Run/Cancel checkboxes
4. Pre-check boxes based on existing group.clusterPermissions
5. Allow adding new clusters or modifying existing permissions
6. Allow removing clusters by unchecking all actions

**Submission:**
- Build updated clusterPermissions object
- Call updateGroup(groupName, { description, clusterPermissions })
- PATCH only sends changed fields (description?, clusterPermissions?)
- On success: close modal, refresh groups list
- On error: show validation errors

**Validation:**
- Description optional
- At least one cluster must have at least one action (can't remove all)

**Edge cases:**
- Handle clusters that were removed from targets but still in group permissions
- Show warning: 'Cluster X no longer exists in targets'
- Allow removing orphaned cluster permissions

**UI/UX:**
- Clear visual difference between 'existing' and 'new' cluster selections
- Confirm dialog if removing all permissions from a cluster

title: Add API service for active runs dashboard endpoint
description: Create API service method to call GET /api/v1/dashboard/active-runs with JWT auth. Returns totalActiveRuns, totalClusters, and clusterRuns map.

title: Integrate ActiveRunsSummary into JobsList component
description: Add ActiveRunsSummary component above the jobs table in JobsList. Use useActiveRunsPoller hook. Handle loading and error states gracefully. Position summary with proper spacing.

title: Fix failing React tests in console
description:
Fix all failing tests in krkn-operator-console:
1. UserManagement: Edit/Delete buttons not found
2. UserForm: active field handling, password field null
3. UserDetails: Status always shows Inactive
4. usersApi: Update user test fails

All tests must pass before CI/CD is fully functional.
close_reason: All 88 tests passing. Fixed UserDetails, UserForm, UserManagement, and usersApi tests. Main issues: field name mismatches (enabled->active, createdAt->created), dropdown menu interactions, and HTTP method (PUT->PATCH).

title: Add home link to krkn-operator-console title in header
description:
Add clickable link to homepage when clicking on 'krkn-operator-console' title in the application header.

**Current State:**
- Title is static text in header
- No way to quickly navigate back to home

**Requirements:**
- Make title clickable
- Navigate to '/' (home) on click
- Use React Router Link component
- Maintain current styling

**Files to Modify:**
- src/App.tsx (or wherever header/navbar is defined)

**Implementation:**
```tsx
import { Link } from 'react-router-dom';

<Link to="/" style={{ textDecoration: 'none', color: 'inherit' }}>
  krkn-operator-console
</Link>
```

title: Implement smart polling pause for open scenario run accordions
description:
When user opens a scenario run accordion to view logs, pause polling for that specific run to prevent log interruptions. Add visual indicator (banner + manual refresh button) so user knows updates are paused. Auto-resume when accordion closes.

Implementation:
1. Modify useScenarioRunsPoller to skip runs in expandedRunIds
2. Add state to track which runs have paused polling
3. Add Alert banner in JobsList accordion when polling paused
4. Add manual refresh button in banner
5. Ensure polling auto-resumes when accordion closes

Benefits:
- Zero interruptions while viewing logs
- Clear UX with informative banner
- Manual refresh option for user control
- Auto-resume when done viewing

title: Create ActiveRunsModal with tree view
description: PatternFly Modal component showing tree structure (max depth 1): cluster names as parent nodes, scenario run names as children. Use DataList or TreeView component. Include close button.

title: Rename 'User Settings' page to 'Users & Groups'
description: Update page title, navigation, and route name from 'User Settings' to 'Users & Groups' to reflect new dual functionality.

title: Debug and fix persistent WebSocket duplication in StrictMode
description: StrictMode guard not preventing duplication. Need to investigate: 1) Check if cleanup runs before second mount, 2) Verify wsRef state during second mount, 3) Consider alternative patterns like ignore flag or connection tracking

title: Implement Groups management UI in console
description: Add Groups management to User Settings page (rename to Users & Groups). Two-card layout: Groups (top) and Users (bottom). Groups card shows list of groups with CRUD operations. Users cannot be created until at least one group exists.

title: Fix login redirect loop causing session expired warning
description:
Login flow had duplicate navigate() calls causing race condition:
- handleSubmit called navigate('/app') after login
- useEffect also called navigate('/app') when state changed
- This caused redirect loop showing 'Session Expired' warning

Fixed by removing navigate() from handleSubmit and letting only useEffect handle redirect.

Commit: 108bc63

title: Create GroupsCard component with table and actions
description:
Create GroupsCard component to display above existing UsersCard.

**Layout:**
- Card title: 'Groups'
- Button: 'Create Group' (top-right, triggers CreateGroupModal)
- Table showing all groups

**Table Columns:**
1. Name (string, primary identifier)
2. Description (string, truncate if long)
3. Member Count (number, e.g., '5 members')
4. Clusters (number or badges, e.g., '3 clusters' or cluster badges)
5. Actions (Edit, Delete buttons)

**Data Loading:**
- On mount: call listGroups()
- Use React Query for caching/refetching
- Show loading skeleton while fetching
- Show empty state if no groups: 'No groups yet. Create your first group to get started.'

**Actions:**
- Edit button → opens EditGroupModal with groupName
- Delete button → confirmation dialog → deleteGroup(name) → refresh list
  - Warning: 'Are you sure? This will remove all users from this group.'
  - If group has members, show count: 'This group has 5 members who will be removed.'

**Clicking row:**
- Optional: click row to expand inline details (cluster list with permissions)
- Or: click row to open ViewGroupModal (read-only details)

**State Management:**
- Pass groups data down to UsersCard for validation (disable Create User if groups.length === 0)
- Refresh groups list after create/edit/delete

**UI/UX:**
- Use Patternfly Table component
- Sortable by Name, Member Count
- Filterable by name (search input)
- Responsive: stack columns on mobile

title: Add user creation validation: require at least one group
description:
Prevent user creation when no groups exist.

**Implementation:**
1. In UsersCard component, receive groups array from parent (Users & Groups page)
2. Check groups.length === 0
3. If no groups:
   - Disable 'Create User' button
   - Add Tooltip: 'Create at least one group before adding users'
   - Optionally: show alert/banner above users table:
     'Users must belong to at least one group. Create a group first.'

4. If groups.length > 0:
   - Enable button normally

**Edge Case:**
- User deletes last group while CreateUserModal is open
  - Modal should detect this and show error
  - Or: block group deletion if it's the only group and has users

**Alternative Validation:**
- Allow creating users without groups initially
- But require group selection in CreateUserModal (validation error if none selected)
- This requires updating API to not enforce group membership on user creation

**Recommended Approach:**
- Block user creation UI if no groups (clearer UX)
- Enforce group membership in CreateUserModal (at least one group required)
- Backend already handles group assignment via labels

**UI/UX:**
- Disabled button with clear tooltip is enough
- Don't need intrusive modal/alert
- Groups card is visible above, user can see it's empty

title: Add group membership management UI (view/add/remove members)
description:
Add group membership management UI.

**Entry Point:**
- Add 'Members' action/link in GroupsCard table
- Or: click member count to open members modal

**ViewGroupMembersModal:**
1. Title: 'Group Members - {groupName}'
2. On open: fetch GET /api/v1/groups/:groupName/members
3. Display members table:
   - Columns: User ID (email), Name, Surname, Role, Remove Action
   - Show member count in subtitle: '{count} members'
4. Button: 'Add Members' (opens AddMembersModal)
5. Remove button per member → confirmation → removeGroupMember(groupName, userId)

**AddMembersModal (nested or separate):**
1. Fetch GET /api/v1/users to get all users
2. Filter out users already in this group (compare with current members)
3. Show multi-select list/table of available users
4. User can select multiple users to add
5. Submit → call addGroupMember(groupName, userId) for each selected user
   - Handle parallel requests or sequential
   - Show progress indicator
6. On success: close modal, refresh members list

**Edge Cases:**
- No users available to add: 'All users are already in this group'
- No users exist yet: 'Create users first before adding them to groups'
- API error on add: show which users failed, allow retry
- Concurrent modification: refresh members list if stale

**Validation:**
- Can remove all members from group (group can be empty)
- Cannot add user that's already in group (filtered in UI)
- Removal requires confirmation: 'Remove {userName} from {groupName}?'

**UI/UX:**
- Use Patternfly Modal > nested Modal for add
- Show loading states during API calls
- Success toast: 'Added 3 users to dev-team'
- Error toast: 'Failed to add 2 users' with retry option
- Optimistic updates: add/remove immediately in UI, rollback on error

title: Refactor group modals to use async cluster discovery flow
description: Replace direct targetsApi.listTargets() calls in CreateGroupModal and EditGroupModal with proper async flow: POST /api/v1/targets → poll GET /api/v1/targets/{uuid} → GET /api/v1/clusters?id={uuid}. Create reusable useClusterDiscovery hook that shows spinner during loading and supports multi-operator sources (krkn-operator, krkn-operator-acm).
close_reason: Implemented async cluster discovery hook for group modals. Replaces direct targetsApi calls with proper POST /targets → poll → GET /clusters flow. Shows spinner during loading, supports multiple operators, handles errors with retry. All tests passing.
